Description: Paroxysmal positional vertigo is a sudden, brief episode of dizziness triggered by specific head movements. It's the most common inner ear disorder causing vertigo. While there is no specific diet for PPV, some people find that avoiding caffeine and alcohol can help reduce symptoms because they can dehydrate you. Meniere's disease, which can cause PPV episodes, may benefit from a low-salt diet.

Diet: Limit processed foods, sugary drinks, and refined carbohydratesChoose lean protein sourcesEat plenty of fruits, vegetables, and whole grainsDrink plenty of water
Description: Acne is a common skin condition that involves the clogging of hair follicles by dead skin cells and oil. Although diet isn't considered a direct cause of acne, some foods can worsen breakouts, especially those high in refined carbohydrates and sugar. Here are some dietary tips that may help with acne:

Diet: Eat lean protein such as skinless chicken, turkey, fish, egg whites, tofu and beans. Use cooking techniques that are low in fat, such as baking, broiling or sautéing. Focus on whole grain products such as whole wheat breads, pastas and cereals, brown rice, barley or quinoa.
Description: Acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS), is an ongoing, also called chronic, condition. It's caused by the human immunodeficiency virus, also called HIV. HIV damages the immune system so that the body is less able to fight infection and disease.

Diet: Avoid alcohol completely, Eat a balanced diet rich in fruits, vegetables, and whole grains. Choose lean protein sources. Limit processed foods, sugary drinks, and unhealthy fats
Description: Alcoholic hepatitis is an inflammation of the liver caused by excessive alcohol consumption. In severe cases, it can lead to liver failure. There is no specific diet to cure alcoholic hepatitis, but a healthy diet is crucial for promoting liver healing and preventing further damage. Here are some dietary recommendations for people with alcoholic hepatitis:

Diet: Diet should include anti-inflammatory foods like fruits, vegetables, and omega-3 fatty acids. Avoid known allergens.
Description: An allergy is an immune system reaction to a substance in the environment that is harmless to most people. These substances are called allergens and can be found in many things

Diet: There is no specific diet proven to cure or prevent arthritis, but some dietary changes may help manage symptoms. Here are some recommendations:Maintain a healthy weight: Excess weight puts stress on your joints, so losing weight if you are overweight or obese can help reduce pain and improve mobility. Eat a balanced diet that includes plenty of fruits, vegetables, and whole grains. These foods are rich in antioxidants and nutrients that can help reduce inflammation. Choose lean protein sources, such as fish, chicken, and beans.Limit unhealthy fats, such as saturated and trans fats, which can increase inflammation. Consider adding omega-3 fatty acids to your diet. Omega-3s have anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce joint pain. Talk to your doctor about whether fish oil supplements might be beneficial.
Description: Arthritis is an umbrella term for a group of conditions that cause joint pain, inflammation, and stiffness. There are over 100 different types of arthritis, with the most common being osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. Osteoarthritis is caused by wear and tear on the joints, while rheumatoid arthritis is an autoimmune disease that attacks the joints. Symptoms of arthritis can vary depending on the type, but may include joint pain, swelling, stiffness, and difficulty moving the joints.

Diet: There is no specific diet for asthma, but some people find that certain foods can trigger their symptoms. If you have asthma, it's important to identify and avoid your triggers. Some common asthma triggers include:Dust mites, Pet dander, Pollen, Mold, Smoke,Pollution, Certain medications,such as aspirin and ibuprofen. Sulfites, which are found in some processed foods and wines, Food allergies
Description: Bronchial asthma, often referred to simply as asthma, is a chronic inflammatory disease of the airways. It causes airways to become narrowed and inflamed, which leads to wheezing, coughing, shortness of breath, and chest tightness. Asthma attacks can be triggered by a variety of factors, including allergens, irritants, exercise, and respiratory infections.

Diet: Vitamins A and C help reduce inflammation so one must consume lots of fresh fruits and veggies. Other nutrients important for reducing inflammation are omega-3 fatty acids and Vitamin E such as oilseeds, nuts and fish. One must avoid highly acidic foods like red meat, citrus fruits and even white potatoes.
Description: Cervical spondylosis is a type of arthritis that affects the bones and joints in the neck (cervical spine). It is a degenerative condition that occurs as we age. The discs between the vertebrae in the neck can become dehydrated and lose height. The bones may also develop bone spurs (osteophytes). These changes can narrow the spinal canal and compress the spinal cord and nerves. Symptoms of cervical spondylosis can vary depending on the severity of the condition, but may include neck pain, stiffness, headaches, and radiating pain into the arms or shoulders.

Diet: Spicy foods: Peppers, hot sauces, salsa, garlic Acidic foods: Grapes, pineapples, tomatoes, citrus fruits and juices, pickled foods, coffee
Description: Chickenpox, also known as varicella, is a highly contagious viral infection that causes a rash with itchy blisters. It's caused by the varicella-zoster virus, which is part of the herpesvirus family.

Diet: Chronic cholestasis: Diet should focus on reducing fat intake and promoting liver health. Include high-fiber foods and healthy fats like omega-3 fatty acids.
Common Cold: Diet should include immune-boosting foods like fruits, vegetables, and lean proteins. Stay hydrated and rest.

Description: Dengue viruses are spread to people through the bite of an infected Aedes species (Ae. aegypti or Ae. albopictus) mosquito. Almost half of the world's population, about 4 billion people, live in areas with a risk of dengue. Dengue is often a leading cause of illness in areas with risk.

Diet: Diabetes: Diet should focus on controlling blood sugar levels. Include whole grains, fruits, vegetables, lean proteins, and limit sugar and refined carbohydrates.
Description: Diabetes is a chronic disease that occurs either when the pancreas does not produce enough insulin or when the body cannot effectively use the insulin it produces. Insulin is a hormone that regulates blood glucose.

Diet: A high-fiber diet can help treat and prevent hemorrhoids
Description: Piles rarely cause any serious problems but sometimes they can lead to the following. External piles (swellings that develop further down your anal canal, closer to your anus) can become inflamed and swollen; ulcers can also form on them. Skin tags can form when the inside of a pile shrinks back but the skin remains.

Diet: Whole-grain flours, buckwheat, stone-ground cornmeal, and rye contain insoluble fiber. Cooked oats and barley contain soluble fiber.Legumes Beans, lentils, and nuts contain both soluble and insoluble fiber. Fruits and vegetablesThin-skinned fruits and vegetables, like apples, pears, plums, and potatoes, contain insoluble fiber. Enough water makes your bowel movement smooth and regular.

Fungal infections are any disease or condition you get from a fungus. They usually affect your skin, hair, nails or mucous membranes but they can also infect your lungs or other parts of your body. You're at higher risk for fungal infections if you have a weakened immune system.

Description: Gastroenteritis is inflammation of the stomach, small intestine, or large intestine, leading to a combination of abdominal pain, cramping, nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Acute gastroenteritis usually lasts fewer than 14 days

Diet: GERD (Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease): Diet should include low-acid foods, avoid spicy and acidic foods, and eat smaller meals. Avoid alcohol and caffeine.
Description: Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) is a common condition in which the stomach contents move up into the esophagus. Reflux becomes a disease when it causes frequent or severe symptoms or injury. Reflux may damage the esophagus, pharynx or respiratory tract

Diet: Heart attack: Diet should focus on heart health and include fruits, vegetables, whole grains, lean proteins, and healthy fats like omega-3 fatty acids. Limit saturated fats and sodium.
Description: A heart attack, also called a myocardial infarction, happens when a part of the heart muscle doesn't get enough blood. The more time that passes without treatment to restore blood flow, the greater the damage to the heart muscle. Coronary artery disease (CAD) is the main cause of heart attack.

Diet: Hepatitis (A): Diet should focus on promoting liver health and include low-fat, high-fiber foods. Avoid alcohol and limit sodium intake.
Description: Hepatitis A is a contagious liver infection caused by the hepatitis A virus (HAV). It's a type of hepatitis virus that causes inflammation of the liver and affects its function

Diet: Hepatitis (B): Diet should focus on promoting liver health and include low-fat, high-fiber foods. Avoid alcohol and limit sodium intake.
Description: Hepatitis B can be acute or chronic. Acute hepatitis B is a short-term illness that occurs within the first 6 months after exposure to the virus. Chronic hepatitis B lasts more than six months and is lifelong

Diet: Hepatitis (C): Diet should focus on promoting liver health and include low-fat, high-fiber foods. Avoid alcohol and limit sodium intake.
Description: Hepatitis C is a liver infection caused by the hepatitis C virus. Hepatitis C can range from a mild illness lasting a few weeks to a serious, long-term illness. Hepatitis C is often described as “acute,” meaning a new infection, or “chronic,” meaning long-term infection.

Diet: Hepatitis (D): Diet should focus on promoting liver health and include low-fat, high-fiber foods. Avoid alcohol and limit sodium intake.
Description: Hepatitis D is a viral infection that causes liver inflammation and damage. Inflammation is swelling that occurs when tissues of the body become injured or infected. Inflammation can damage organs.

Diet: Hepatitis (E): Diet should focus on promoting liver health and include low-fat, high-fiber foods. Avoid alcohol and limit sodium intake.
Description: Hepatitis E is a liver disease caused by the hepatitis E virus (HEV). Although rare in developed countries, hepatitis E is widespread in the developing world. In the vast majority of people, HEV infection results in a self-limited, acute illness.

Diet: Hypertension: Diet should focus on reducing sodium intake and include potassium-rich foods like fruits and vegetables. Also, emphasize whole grains, lean proteins, and healthy fats.
Description: Hypertension (high blood pressure) is when the pressure in your blood vessels is too high (140/90 mmHg or higher). It is common but can be serious if not treated. People with high blood pressure may not feel symptoms. The only way to know is to get your blood pressure checked.

Diet: Hyperthyroidism: Diet should focus on limiting iodine intake and avoiding stimulants like caffeine. Include calcium-rich foods and foods that support bone health.
Description: Hyperthyroidism happens when the thyroid gland makes too much thyroid hormone. This condition also is called overactive thyroid. Hyperthyroidism speeds up the body's metabolism. That can cause many symptoms, such as weight loss, hand tremors, and rapid or irregular heartbeat.

Diet: A diet for hypoglycemia can include, Small, frequent meals, A variety of foods, Lean protein, Complex carbohydrates, Foods high in soluble fiber, Healthy fats, A small amount of fast-acting carbohydrates
Description: Hypoglycemia means that your blood sugar (glucose) level is lower than the standard range. It's often related to diabetes treatment, but other drugs and a variety of conditions can cause low blood sugar

Diet: Hypothyroidism: Diet should include iodine-rich foods like seafood, as well as selenium-rich foods like Brazil nuts. Limit goitrogenic foods like cabbage and soy.
Description: Hypothyroidism, also called underactive thyroid, is when the thyroid gland doesn't make enough thyroid hormones to meet your body's needs. The thyroid is a small, butterfly-shaped gland in the front of your neck.

Diet: Impetigo: Diet should focus on supporting the immune system with nutrient-dense foods like fruits, vegetables, and lean proteins.
Description: Impetigo starts as a red, itchy sore. As it heals, a crusty, yellow or “honey-colored” scab forms over the sore. In general, impetigo is a mild infection that can occur anywhere on the body. It most often affects exposed skin, such as. Around the nose and mouth.

Diet: Jaundice: Diet should focus on liver health and include easily digestible foods like fruits, vegetables, and lean proteins. Avoid fatty and fried foods.
Description: A condition in which the skin and the whites of the eyes become yellow, urine darkens, and the color of stool becomes lighter than normal. Jaundice occurs when the liver is not working properly or when a bile duct is blocked.

Diet: Fresh fruits and vegetables work wonders for malaria patients. According to studies, vitamin A and vitamin C rich fruits and vegetables like beetroot, carrot, papaya, sweet lime, grapes, berries, lemon, orange help to detoxify and boost the immunity of the patient suffering from malaria.
Description: Malaria is a life-threatening disease caused by parasites transmitted by the bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes. It is mostly found in tropical countries, especially in Africa South of the Sahara and in parts of Oceania such as Papua New Guinea

Diet: Migraine: Diet should avoid known trigger foods like aged cheeses, chocolate, and processed meats. Stay hydrated and eat regular meals.
Description: A migraine is a headache that can cause severe throbbing pain or a pulsing sensation, usually on one side of the head. It's often accompanied by nausea, vomiting, and extreme sensitivity to light and sound.

Diet: Osteoarthritis: Diet should focus on anti-inflammatory foods like fruits, vegetables, omega-3 fatty acids, and lean proteins. Maintain a healthy weight.
Description: Osteoarthritis is a degenerative joint disease, in which the tissues in the joint break down over time. It is the most common type of arthritis and is more common in older people. People with osteoarthritis usually have joint pain and, after rest or inactivity, stiffness for a short period of time.

Diet: Plenty of vegetables of different types and colours, legumes and beans. Fruit. Grain (cereal) foods, mostly wholegrain and high fibre varieties such as breads, cereals, rice, pasta, noodles, polenta, couscous, oats, quinoa and barley. Lean meats and poultry, fish, eggs, tofu, nuts, seeds, legumes and beans.
Description: The symptoms of a brain bleed, or intracranial hemorrhage, differ depending on which part of the brain is affected. Common brain bleeds symptoms include: Weakness, numbness, tingling, and facial paralysis. Often these symptoms affect the arm and leg on one side of the body

Diet: aim to have a diet high in fibre and rich in vegetables, fruits, and whole grains. Try for a minimum of seven servings of vegetables and fruits each day, and a minimum of five servings of whole grains
Description: Peptic ulcer disease (PUD) is a condition that causes open sores to develop in the stomach or duodenum, the first part of the small intestine

Diet: Pneumonia: Diet should include nutrient-dense foods to support recovery. Stay hydrated and eat small, frequent meals.
Description: Pneumonia is an infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs. The air sacs may fill with fluid or pus (purulent material), causing cough with phlegm or pus, fever, chills, and difficulty breathing. A variety of organisms, including bacteria, viruses and fungi, can cause pneumonia.

Diet: Psoriasis: Diet should include anti-inflammatory foods like fruits, vegetables, and omega-3 fatty acids. Limit alcohol and processed foods.
Description: Psoriasis is a skin disease that causes a rash with itchy, scaly patches, most commonly on the knees, elbows, trunk and scalp. Psoriasis is a common, long-term (chronic) disease with no cure.

Diet: Tuberculosis: Diet should include nutrient-dense foods to boost the immune system. Include protein-rich foods and foods rich in vitamins A, C, and E.
Description: Tuberculosis (TB) is a disease caused by germs that are spread from person to person through the air. TB usually affects the lungs, but it can also affect other parts of the body, such as the brain, the kidneys, or the spine.

Diet: high-calorie diet, food items such as bananas, potatoes etc. Up the consumption of dairy products such as milk and yogurt in your typhoid diet. Soups such as spinach soup, vegetable soup, carrot soup, chicken soup, and mushroom soup
Description: Typhoid fever is a bacterial infection that can spread throughout the body, affecting many organs. Without prompt treatment, it can cause serious complications and can be fatal. It's caused by a bacterium called Salmonella typhi, which is related to the bacteria that cause salmonella food poisoning.

Diet: Urinary tract infection: Diet should focus on staying hydrated and include foods that promote urinary health like cranberries and probiotics.
Description: UTIs are common infections that happen when bacteria, often from the skin or rectum, enter the urethra, and infect the urinary tract. The infections can affect several parts of the urinary tract, but the most common type is a bladder infection (cystitis). Kidney infection (pyelonephritis) is another type of UTI.

Diet: Dark leafy green, Vitamin C-rich foods, Vitamin E-rich foods,Potassium-rich foods,Foods high in omega-3
Description: Varicose veins are bulging, enlarged veins. Any vein that is close to the skin's surface, called superficial, can become varicosed. Varicose veins most often affect the veins in the legs. That's because standing and walking increase the pressure in the veins of the lower body.
